title: Nigel Gebodh
text:
I’m a PhD candidate in Biomedical Engineering and an NIH-GRISE and Grove School of Engineering Fellow.
My work is at the intersection of digital health, machine learning, wearables, biosignals, and understanding brain and body connections.
I currently work on building, understanding, and integrating intelligent systems with wearables and digital health applications.

title: Nigel Gebodh
text:
I’m a PhD candidate in Biomedical Engineering and an NIH-GRISE and Grove School of Engineering Fellow.

title: Nigel Gebodh
text:
Select Publications

A Scalable Framework for Closed-Loop Neuromodulation with Deep Learning. Nigel Gebodh, Vladimir Miskovic, Sarah Laszlo, Abhishek Datta, Marom Bikson. bioRxiv 2023.01.18.524615; doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.01.18.524615

See all publications here.

title: Nigel Gebodh
text:
Education
The Grove School of Engineering, The City College of New York, CUNY

PhD & MPhil
Building improved wearables, and assessing neural (sleep/attention/vigilance) and physiological function (EEG, ECG, EMG etc.) under brain stimulation with computational modeling, and machine learning.


MSci
Understanding the human visual system by mapping the early visual cortex. Applying signal detection and signal processing techniques.


BE
Medical device design, device manufacturing, and rapid prototyping.

title: Welcome To My Blog
text:
This is the first post in a Quarto blog. Welcome!

Since this post doesn’t specify an explicit image, the first image in the post will be used in the listing page of posts.
